title: Phosphate Buffered Saline (PBS)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"1j2vv","text":"A buffer solution has the function of resisting changes in pH even when adding powerful acids or bases. However, in the physiological environment the buffered system also provides cofactors for enzymatic reactions, critical salts and even essential nutrients for cells and tissues. Therefore, when trying to reproduce biological conditions in vitro, we must make the appropriate choice of the buffer. After all, it will provide the appropriate medium in which reactions will occur.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}

warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"1m8jr","text":"Wear personal protective equipment: gloves, lab coat and mask.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: pH 7.4 150 mM NaCl 10 mM Potassium Phosphate buffer Prepare 1 L, PBS by dissolving 8.7 g, NaCl , 1.82 g, K2HPO4 • 3H2O and 0.23 g, KH2PO4 in 1 L, distilled water .
A variation of PBS can also be prepared as follows: 137 millimolar (mM), NaCl 2.7 millimolar (mM), KCl 10 millimolar (mM), Na2HPO4 1.76 millimolar (mM), KH2PO4
creator: Neilier Junior
